TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no daemon code change identified; the failure is infrastructure-side log stream connectivity.
• Is it a flake? Yes
• Suggested Fix: Retry GetPodLogsRegex with exponential backoff on context deadline exceeded / stream errors (already partially attempted per ptphelper.go:123 retry log) and add a SinceSeconds window to avoid streaming large log volumes. Also investigate whether the telco5g bare-metal cluster's API server → kubelet log proxy connections are being cut by an intermediate timeout (check keepalive/idle settings on the cluster's internal load balancer).

Suite Issue Class: infra_timeout

Suite Severity: MEDIUM


dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests timed out because the test helper GetProfileLogID could not find the Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1 log marker in linuxptp-daemon-9cv64's container logs — that entry was rotated out ~40 minutes before the tests ran.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Profile log entry lost to container log rotation (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)

• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-9cv64 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded [tests 1 & 2]


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-9cv64 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=timedout waiting for matches [test 3]

```

Test wall-clock timeouts (~201 s, ~205 s, ~207 s) are consistent with a 3-minute GetProfileLogID Pass-2 follow timeout (180 s) plus overhead. The dualfollower suite started at 05:45:01; the operator/daemon was deployed at ~05:05:33 — a gap of ~40 minutes.


The same failure pattern (could not get any profile line) hits every other suite in this run (bc → linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8/fpgx7; dualnicbc → linuxptp-daemon-bvs6q/4cmtj; dualnicbcha → linuxptp-daemon-8fk9k/mt258). All are cross-suite, cross-pod, and trigger the identical error path, confirming a systemic cause.


• Root Cause: BasicClockSyncCheck in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:60 calls GetClockIDForeign → GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69), which scans linuxptp-daemon-container logs for the regex:

```

(?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.*)\]

```

The daemon emits this line exactly once — inside applyNodePtpProfile → printNodeProfile → printWhenNotNil(nodeProfile.Ptp4lConf, "Ptp4lConf") at daemon.go:1369, called only when a ptpconfig is (re)applied. By 05:45, ptp4l + phc2sys have logged ~1 line/s for ~40 minutes; the early Ptp4lConf: entry has been rotated out of the container log visible to the Kubernetes GetLogs API. GetPodLogsRegex Pass 1 (snapshot, test/pkg/pods/pods.go:214) finds nothing; Pass 2 (follow, 3-minute context at pods.go:254) waits fruitlessly because the daemon never re-logs the profile until reconfigured, exhausting the timeout (pods.go:153 for tests 1–2, pods.go:167 for test 3), which bubbles up as ptphelper.go:83 "could not get any profile line".


• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the log-rotation exposure is a pre-existing test design limitation; no single PR is directly identifiable from available evidence. The most recent structural changes near this code path are 9be567c (2026-05-07, "refactor: Remove unused functions and enhance PTP configuration logic") and 0f98a60 (2026-01-14, "fixed ci blocker: added context timeout to GetLogsPodRegex") on the ptp-operator main branch; either could have affected how long Pass-1/Pass-2 tolerates missing entries. The release-4.20 branch merge history is not available locally to confirm backport.

• Is it a flake? No — all 3 tests in this suite failed, and the exact same pattern repeats across every other non-oc suite in the same run. This is a systematic failure.

• Suggested Fix: Change GetProfileLogID to periodically re-trigger the Ptp4lConf: log entry (e.g., by forcing a no-op ptpconfig update to restart reconciliation), or redesign the profile-ID lookup to not rely on a one-shot log event that may be rotated — for example, read it from the live PtpConfig CR spec and derive the messageTag from the known naming scheme (ptp4l..config:{level}), which is deterministic and does not require log scanning.

Suite Issue Class: test_failure

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests timed out because the linuxptp-daemon containers never emitted the selected best master clock log line, indicating PTP synchronization was never achieved on the test cluster nodes.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: linuxptp-daemon not achieving PTP clock synchronization (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-bvs6q container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-4cmtj container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-bvs6q container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

And from the build log post-run (gathering phase):

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

error: creating temp namespace: Unauthorized

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetClockIDForeign() (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:127-151) scans the linuxptp-daemon-container log stream for the regex (?m)\[%s\]. selected best master clock (.) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex() (test/pkg/pods/pods.go:214-269), which internally calls findRegexInStream() (pods.go:141-171) with a 10-minute timeout. This line is only emitted by ptp4l when it successfully selects a grandmaster/best master; since it never appeared in any daemon pod across all ptpconfigs (test-bc-master1, test-slave1, temp), the PTP daemon was not achieving clock synchronization. Critically, the identical "could not find regex in log stream" error hit all ClockSync tests across bc-serial, dualfollower-serial, dualnicbcha-serial, and dualnicbc-serial as well — indicating a cluster-wide failure to synchronize PTP, most likely caused by the telco5g lab's physical grandmaster being unreachable during this CI run rather than a code regression.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the most recent linuxptp-daemon commit is 0459bc0 (upstream-sync-2026-06-22) and the ptp-operator test repo shows 3586177 ("ci: add ginkgo flake mitigation and fix higher-priority test"). No log-format changes were observed that would break the regex. The failure is consistent with an infra/lab connectivity issue.
• Is it a flake? Yes — the cross-suite, cross-pod, cross-node uniformity of the timeout (all suites hit the same pattern) strongly indicates a transient infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, PTP network disruption in the telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com lab cluster) rather than a deterministic code regression. The oc-serial tests that ran later additionally hit Unauthorized / connection refused to the API server (10.8.34.117:6443), further confirming the lab cluster was degraded during this run.
• Suggested Fix: Re-run the nightly job to confir


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. • Suggested Fix: Retry GetPodLogsRegex with exponential backoff on context deadline exceeded / stream errors (already partially attempted per ptphelper.go:123 retry log) and add a SinceSeconds window to avoid streaming large log volumes. Also investigate whether the telco5g bare-metal cluster's API server → kubelet log proxy connections are being cut by an intermediate timeout (check keepalive/idle settings on the cluster's internal load balancer).

Suite: dualnicbc (3 failures)

2026-06-24T05:00
❓ infra
❌ failure4.214.21-e2e-telco5g-ptp184minfra_other LOW
Telco lab cluster (ptpcimno) was unreachable; no PTP tests ran due to infra failure.
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: Telco lab cluster (ptpcimno) was unreachable; no PTP tests ran due to infra failure.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Telco lab cluster not provisioned / API server unreachable (affects: all job steps post-setup)
• Evidence:

```

scp: /root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig: No such file or directory

(cluster-setup step, 2026-06-25T05:19:38Z, exit status 1)


ClusterVersion: Installing "" for :

clusteroperators are missing


dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

(gather-must-gather, gather-extra, gather-audit-logs — all fail hitting 10.8.34.117:6443)

```

• Root Cause: The Ansible playbook fetch-kubeconfig.yml attempted to kcli scp root@ptpcimno-installer:/root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig but the file was absent, indicating the OCP installation on the baremetal lab cluster ptpcimno never completed. The API server at 10.8.34.117:6443 was unreachable for every subsequent step. The cluster-setup step exited with code 1 at 05:19:38Z — all downstream gather steps cascaded from this single infra failure. No PTP-operator code or tests ever ran.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — lab environment state issue, not a code regression.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient lab cluster provisioning failure; no code change triggered it.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if it recurs, inspect the ptpcimno lab cluster provisioning pipeline and verify the installer VM has a completed kubeconfig at /root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig before CI jobs are scheduled against it.
• Affected tests: All steps — e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup (failed), gather-must-gather, gather-extra, gather-audit-logs (all cascaded failures). Zero PTP e2e tests executed.


Relation Between Groups: Single root cause — lab cluster not ready cascades into every subsequent step.


Per-Version Notes:

• 4.19: Confirmed — lab cluster ptpcimno API unreachable, cluster-setup step failed.
• 4.21: Likely same infra root cause if using the same telco lab pool; no separate evidence available.
• 4.23: Likely same infra root cause if using the same telco lab pool; no separate evidence available.

Affected Images: None — image imports all succeeded (registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.19.0-0.nightly-2026-06-24-152413, 187 images imported cleanly). No image pull failures.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: LOW

2026-06-25T00:00
❌ failure4.224.22-e2e-telco5g-ptp261mtest_flake LOW
WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 DPLL never entered HOLDOVER (clock class 7) after GNSS coldboot.
2 failed tests
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 DPLL never entered HOLDOVER (clock class 7) after GNSS coldboot.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: DPLL fails to reach/maintain clock class 7 (HOLDOVER) after GNSS loss (affects Tests: both)
• Evidence:

```

Test 1 — DPLL stuck at FREERUN for full 200s after GNSS disabled:

Matched line: [ptp4l 248] → GNSS disabled → Clock class not yet 7, retrying... (×32+)

→ GNSS enabled → [FAILED] Timed out waiting for clock class 7 (ptp.go:3802, 3m20s elapsed)


Test 2 — class 7 transient, immediately overtaken by 248:

PtpClockClassChange Event recieved map[metric:6], map[metric:7], map[metric:248]

GnssStateChange Event recieved map[metric:0 notification:FAILURE-NOFIX]

→ STEP: Verifying ClockClass value to 7 → [FAILED] expected a metric [7.000000] but got map[metric:248] (ptp.go:3898)

```

• Root Cause: After GNSS signal loss on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, the WPC hardware DPLL transitions directly from LOCKED to FREERUN (class 248) without sustaining HOLDOVER (class 7). In pkg/event/event.go, clock class 7 requires dpllState == PTP_HOLDOVER; since the DPLL reports FREERUN immediately, that path is never taken. In test 2, HOLDOVER was briefly entered (class 7 visible in the event stream) but exhausted faster than the metric-polling step could verify it — confirming the DPLL holdover window on this hardware is abnormally short.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the upstream sync PR #613 (0459bc0, CNF-24996/OCPBUGS-90708, merged 2026-06-23) was an upstream sync to the main branch and does not directly affect the release-4.22 branch under test; no code regression identified.
• Is it a flake? Yes — test 2 shows holdover does occur but transiently; this is a hardware-timing sensitivity, consistent with intermittent failures when DPLL holdover duration is at the margins.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate DPLL holdover configuration on cnfdf32 (check LocalHoldoverTimeout configured for the WPC hardware); if hardware is degraded or misconfigured for holdover, reprovisioning or reconfiguring the node should resolve it. Retry the job to confirm transience.
• Affected tests:
• [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
• [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]


Relation Between Groups: Single group — both failures share the same root cause: DPLL on cnfdf32 not sustaining HOLDOVER state long enough after GNSS loss. Test 1 never sees class 7 at all; test 2 sees it transiently. Both are direct manifestations of the same abnormally short holdover window on this hardware.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.22 (nightly-2026-06-23-164229) — both failures in the [tgm-serial] suite only; all other suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc) show 0 failures and passed cleanly.


Affected Images: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.22, image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22, image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.22

Overall Severity: LOW

2026-06-25T12:30
❌ failure4.234.23-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream165minfra_other LOW
Telco lab cluster (ptpcimno) was unreachable; no PTP tests ran due to infra failure.
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: Telco lab cluster (ptpcimno) was unreachable; no PTP tests ran due to infra failure.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Telco lab cluster not provisioned / API server unreachable (affects: all job steps post-setup)
• Evidence:

```

scp: /root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig: No such file or directory

(cluster-setup step, 2026-06-25T05:19:38Z, exit status 1)


ClusterVersion: Installing "" for :

clusteroperators are missing


dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

(gather-must-gather, gather-extra, gather-audit-logs — all fail hitting 10.8.34.117:6443)

```

• Root Cause: The Ansible playbook fetch-kubeconfig.yml attempted to kcli scp root@ptpcimno-installer:/root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig but the file was absent, indicating the OCP installation on the baremetal lab cluster ptpcimno never completed. The API server at 10.8.34.117:6443 was unreachable for every subsequent step. The cluster-setup step exited with code 1 at 05:19:38Z — all downstream gather steps cascaded from this single infra failure. No PTP-operator code or tests ever ran.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — lab environment state issue, not a code regression.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient lab cluster provisioning failure; no code change triggered it.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if it recurs, inspect the ptpcimno lab cluster provisioning pipeline and verify the installer VM has a completed kubeconfig at /root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig before CI jobs are scheduled against it.
• Affected tests: All steps — e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup (failed), gather-must-gather, gather-extra, gather-audit-logs (all cascaded failures). Zero PTP e2e tests executed.


Relation Between Groups: Single root cause — lab cluster not ready cascades into every subsequent step.


Per-Version Notes:

• 4.19: Confirmed — lab cluster ptpcimno API unreachable, cluster-setup step failed.
• 4.21: Likely same infra root cause if using the same telco lab pool; no separate evidence available.
• 4.23: Likely same infra root cause if using the same telco lab pool; no separate evidence available.

Affected Images: None — image imports all succeeded (registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.19.0-0.nightly-2026-06-24-152413, 187 images imported cleanly). No image pull failures.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: LOW

2026-06-21T05:00
⚠️ Test Failures
❌ failure5.05.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp372mtest_flake` (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + `test_regression` (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync `0459bc0`) CRITICAL
WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.
25 failed tests
TEST MEDIUM
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available op[tions: ...]

```

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic failure, not random jitter.
• Suggested Fix: Verify the T-GM node achieves GNSS lock (clock class 6) before running downstream sync tests; also ensure the linuxptp-daemon and cloud-event-proxy images built from release-5.0 include the fixes from OCPBUGS-90708 (pmc/ptp4l management interface fix) and OCPBUGS-88369 (CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs), both of which were merged after this job ran.

Suite Issue Class: test_failure

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (9 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 9 failures stem from two independent root causes: pmc being invoked with split arguments causing PARENT_DATA_SET queries to return "bad command" errors, and the BC node's ptp4l stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248) due to an unlocked upstream GM — with event-framework consumer timeouts as a downstream consequence.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PMC "bad command" for PARENT_DATA_SET (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

and

```

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

• Root Cause: The pmc tool is being invoked with "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate argv elements rather than as the single combined string "GET PARENT_DATA_SET". In non-interactive (batch) mode, linuxptp's pmc.c calls do_command(argv[optind]) for each remaining argv item; processing "GET" alone yields "bad command: GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" alone yields "bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET". Other pmc commands (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET used by the clock-class test) still work, confirming ptp4l itself is up and the socket is reachable — only the argument quoting for PARENT_DATA_SET queries is broken. The new non-interactive helper RunPMCGetParentDS in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/pmc/pmc.go:294 passes the full "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" string as one exec.Command arg (which should be fine via sscanf tokenizing). The split-arg pattern strongly implicates the test helper in ptp-operator-conformance-test calling pmc ... GET PARENT_DATA_SET without quoting, or a behavior change in the RHEL 10 linuxptp pmc binary that now treats the space-separated arg as two tokens.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0 — "upstream-sync-2026-06-22", merged 2026-06-22) which synced upstream linuxptp changes into release-5.0; the pmc binary on RHEL 10 / RHCOS 10.2 may have changed its batch-mode argument-parsing behaviour. Test code path: /tmp/cnf-C4K0w/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go (not cloned, exact commit unknown).
• Is it a flake? No — identical error appears verbatim across dualnicbc AND dualnicbcha suites in the same run, showing systematic breakage.
• Suggested Fix: Ensure pmc is invoked with the action and management-ID as a single quoted argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", "-u", "-b", "0", "-f", configPath, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET") in Go, or pmc ... "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" in shell). Alternatively, switch all GET PARENT_DATA_SET queries to the interactive expect-based RunPMCExpGetParentDS (linuxptp-daemon/pkg/pmc/pmc.go:132) which uses e.Send("GET PARENT_DATA_SET\n") and is known to work.

• Group 2: BC ptp4l stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248) (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on dual NIC boundary clock", "PTP metric is present verifies on slave", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

Expected : 248 to equal : 6

```

```

Time metrics are not detected

Expected : # HELP cne_api_events_published ... cne_api_events_published{address="/sync/ptp-status/clock-class",status="success"} 2

```

```

ens2f0np0 should recover to SLAVE

Expected <*errors.errorString>: incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)

```

```

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

```

Cross-corroborating evidence from tgm suite (same CI run):

```

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6) ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM, Expected : false to be true

```

• Root Cause: The WPC Grandmaster node (tgm) itself is not reaching LOCKED state (clock class 6) — demonstrated by multiple tgm suite failures in the same CI run reporting clock class never reaches 6. With the GM in FREERUN/HOLDOVER (class 248), the BC node (cnfdf32, running ptpconfig test-bc-master1 against ptp4l.1.config) cannot lock its upstream port ens2f0np0, causing ptp4l to report class 248. After the network-outage test brings ens2f0np0 down and up, ptp4l without a valid upstream GM elects itself MASTER on that port instead of returning to SLAVE. PTP time-sync metrics (openshift_ptp_clock_state, openshift_ptp_offset_ns) are absent because the linuxptp-daemon does not emit offset/clock-state metrics when not locked; CNE event metrics (cne_api_events_published) are present showing cloud-event-proxy is running but the PTP layer is FREERUN.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — tgm (WPC GNSS/GM hardware) failures point to either a hardware/environment issue on the telco5g lab testbed or a change in the GM node's configuration/firmware. The linuxptp-daemon upstream sync PR #613 (0459bc0, 2026-06-22) is a candidate if it changed ts2phc or WPC GM handling.
• Is it a flake? No — 4 separate tests in dualnicbc plus parallel tests and matching tgm failures all confirm systemic FREERUN state.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate and restore the WPC GM node's GNSS/GPS signal lock (tgm hardware/config), or check whether PR #613's upstream sync changed ts2phc GM behaviour. Once the GM reaches class 6, all BC and slave downstream clock-class/metric/outage-recovery tests should pass.

• Group 3: Cloud-event-proxy PushInitialValue timeout (affects: "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart", "PTP Slave Clock Sync" [parallel])
• Evidence:

```

timedout PushInitialValue, waiting for log in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer, looking for = Got CurrentState: ({.*})

```

```

could not push initial event, err=timedout PushInitialValue, waiting for log in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer, looking for = Got CurrentState: ({.*})

```

• Root Cause: The consumer pod in ptp-event-framework-test never logs "Got CurrentState: ({.*})", meaning it does not receive an initial state push from the cloud-event-proxy sidecar after the linuxptp-daemon pod restart. The most recent cloud-event-proxy commit is fc5d567 — "fix-tbc-dual-publisher-clock-realtime" (PR #701). If this PR changed how dual-NIC BC events are published (e.g., restructuring which publisher emits SyncStateChange for TBC profiles — see cloud-event-proxy/plugins/ptp_operator/metrics/manager.go:388-393), the initial-state push path PushInitialValue may no longer fire on daemon restart for this BC topology. An alternative explanation is that with ptp4l in FREERUN (Group 2), no LOCKED→FREERUN state transition occurs at restart, so no event is generated to push.
• Breaking PR/Commit: cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (fc5d567 — "fix-tbc-dual-publisher-clock-realtime"), merged just before this CI run. Reference: cloud-event-proxy/plugins/ptp_operator/metrics/manager.go:375-393 and metrics/tbc_test.go.
• Is it a flake? No — same timeou


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available op[tions: ...]

```

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic failure, not random jitter.
• Suggested Fix: Verify the T-GM node achieves GNSS lock (clock class 6) before running downstream sync tests; also ensure the linuxptp-daemon and cloud-event-proxy images built from release-5.0 include the fixes from OCPBUGS-90708 (pmc/ptp4l management interface fix) and OCPBUGS-88369 (CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs), both of which were merged after this job ran.

Suite Issue Class: test_failure

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (9 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 9 failures stem from two independent root causes: pmc being invoked with split arguments causing PARENT_DATA_SET queries to return "bad command" errors, and the BC node's ptp4l stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248) due to an unlocked upstream GM — with event-framework consumer timeouts as a downstream consequence.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PMC "bad command" for PARENT_DATA_SET (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

and

```

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

• Root Cause: The pmc tool is being invoked with "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate argv elements rather than as the single combined string "GET PARENT_DATA_SET". In non-interactive (batch) mode, linuxptp's pmc.c calls do_command(argv[optind]) for each remaining argv item; processing "GET" alone yields "bad command: GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" alone yields "bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET". Other pmc commands (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET used by the clock-class test) still work, confirming ptp4l itself is up and the socket is reachable — only the argument quoting for PARENT_DATA_SET queries is broken. The new non-interactive helper RunPMCGetParentDS in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/pmc/pmc.go:294 passes the full "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" string as one exec.Command arg (which should be fine via sscanf tokenizing). The split-arg pattern strongly implicates the test helper in ptp-operator-conformance-test calling pmc ... GET PARENT_DATA_SET without quoting, or a behavior change in the RHEL 10 linuxptp pmc binary that now treats the space-separated arg as two tokens.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0 — "upstream-sync-2026-06-22", merged 2026-06-22) which synced upstream linuxptp changes into release-5.0; the pmc binary on RHEL 10 / RHCOS 10.2 may have changed its batch-mode argument-parsing behaviour. Test code path: /tmp/cnf-C4K0w/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go (not cloned, exact commit unknown).
• Is it a flake? No — identical error appears verbatim across dualnicbc AND dualnicbcha suites in the same run, showing systematic breakage.
• Suggested Fix: Ensure pmc is invoked with the action and management-ID as a single quoted argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", "-u", "-b", "0", "-f", configPath, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET") in Go, or pmc ... "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" in shell). Alternatively, switch all GET PARENT_DATA_SET queries to the interactive expect-based RunPMCExpGetParentDS (linuxptp-daemon/pkg/pmc/pmc.go:132) which uses e.Send("GET PARENT_DATA_SET\n") and is known to work.

• Group 2: BC ptp4l stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248) (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on dual NIC boundary clock", "PTP metric is present verifies on slave", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

Expected : 248 to equal : 6

```

```

Time metrics are not detected

Expected : # HELP cne_api_events_published ... cne_api_events_published{address="/sync/ptp-status/clock-class",status="success"} 2

```

```

ens2f0np0 should recover to SLAVE

Expected <*errors.errorString>: incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)

```

```

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

```

Cross-corroborating evidence from tgm suite (same CI run):

```

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6) ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM, Expected : false to be true

```

• Root Cause: The WPC Grandmaster node (tgm) itself is not reaching LOCKED state (clock class 6) — demonstrated by multiple tgm suite failures in the same CI run reporting clock class never reaches 6. With the GM in FREERUN/HOLDOVER (class 248), the BC node (cnfdf32, running ptpconfig test-bc-master1 against ptp4l.1.config) cannot lock its upstream port ens2f0np0, causing ptp4l to report class 248. After the network-outage test brings ens2f0np0 down and up, ptp4l without a valid upstream GM elects itself MASTER on that port instead of returning to SLAVE. PTP time-sync metrics (openshift_ptp_clock_state, openshift_ptp_offset_ns) are absent because the linuxptp-daemon does not emit offset/clock-state metrics when not locked; CNE event metrics (cne_api_events_published) are present showing cloud-event-proxy is running but the PTP layer is FREERUN.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — tgm (WPC GNSS/GM hardware) failures point to either a hardware/environment issue on the telco5g lab testbed or a change in the GM node's configuration/firmware. The linuxptp-daemon upstream sync PR #613 (0459bc0, 2026-06-22) is a candidate if it changed ts2phc or WPC GM handling.
• Is it a flake? No — 4 separate tests in dualnicbc plus parallel tests and matching tgm failures all confirm systemic FREERUN state.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate and restore the WPC GM node's GNSS/GPS signal lock (tgm hardware/config), or check whether PR #613's upstream sync changed ts2phc GM behaviour. Once the GM reaches class 6, all BC and slave downstream clock-class/metric/outage-recovery tests should pass.

• Group 3: Cloud-event-proxy PushInitialValue timeout (affects: "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart", "PTP Slave Clock Sync" [parallel])
• Evidence:

```

timedout PushInitialValue, waiting for log in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer, looking for = Got CurrentState: ({.*})

```

```

could not push initial event, err=timedout PushInitialValue, waiting for log in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer, looking for = Got CurrentState: ({.*})

```

• Root Cause: The consumer pod in ptp-event-framework-test never logs "Got CurrentState: ({.*})", meaning it does not receive an initial state push from the cloud-event-proxy sidecar after the linuxptp-daemon pod restart. The most recent cloud-event-proxy commit is fc5d567 — "fix-tbc-dual-publisher-clock-realtime" (PR #701). If this PR changed how dual-NIC BC events are published (e.g., restructuring which publisher emits SyncStateChange for TBC profiles — see cloud-event-proxy/plugins/ptp_operator/metrics/manager.go:388-393), the initial-state push path PushInitialValue may no longer fire on daemon restart for this BC topology. An alternative explanation is that with ptp4l in FREERUN (Group 2), no LOCKED→FREERUN state transition occurs at restart, so no event is generated to push.
• Breaking PR/Commit: cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (fc5d567 — "fix-tbc-dual-publisher-clock-realtime"), merged just before this CI run. Reference: cloud-event-proxy/plugins/ptp_operator/metrics/manager.go:375-393 and metrics/tbc_test.go.
• Is it a flake? No — same timeou


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.

• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state.
